title: Beyond Blunderdome

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashes or has accident during opening sequence
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer 'D'oh!' followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Homer's catchphrase is perfectly character-integrated and economical, but it's routine by Season 11

jokes.text: My children deserve to see me get a free gift
jokes.setup: Environmental commercial about leaving clean air for children
jokes.punchline: Homer's motivation is purely selfish - wants the free gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - takes noble environmental message and makes it entirely about his own immediate gratification. Classic subversion of expectations.

jokes.text: You're faking this to get the gift, aren't you? / But I liked the nice things you said about me
jokes.setup: Lisa calls out Homer's obvious insincerity about environmental concerns
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the point and focuses on the compliments
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's selective hearing and need for validation.

jokes.text: What ironment?
jokes.setup: Salesperson mentions people concerned about the environment
jokes.punchline: Homer mispronounces 'environment' as 'ironment'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism. Simple but effective character humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, and it's got a lot of other problems too
jokes.setup: Homer praises the quiet engine making conversation easier
jokes.punchline: Bart deadpan acknowledges the car has many other issues
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with Bart's dry delivery undercutting Homer's misplaced enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Relax. We're in an electric car.
jokes.setup: Dad heading toward harbor while family screams
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks being electric somehow makes driving into water safe
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - completely misunderstands what makes electric cars special in a dangerous situation. Perfect character stupidity.

jokes.text: See? Everything's fine. / Dolphins! / Oopsie. Hi, girls!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming everything is fine after driving into harbor
jokes.punchline: Car electrocuting dolphins, Homer's casual greeting to them
jokes.explanation: Dark visual comedy escalating the car-in-water situation. Homer's casual response to electrocuting marine life is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Salt water seems to be good for it
jokes.setup: Electric car sparking and malfunctioning in salt water
jokes.punchline: Homer concludes salt water is helping the car
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misinterpretation of obvious disaster. Good callback to his earlier confidence.

jokes.text: Help. Help. It burns.
jokes.setup: Car driving away after being returned
jokes.punchline: Electronic voice pleading for help as it burns
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark - the car's AI pleading for mercy as it burns is unexpectedly haunting and funny.

jokes.text: We didn't? That's odd. Seems like we would have done that right after we left the car place. / I know, but we didn't. / Well, here it is. / So we can open it and find out now. / Perfect.
jokes.setup: Family realizing they never opened their gift envelope
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed exposition acknowledging the plot contrivance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy acknowledging lazy plot mechanics. The Simpsons calling out their own writing contrivance.

jokes.text: Mel Gibson! / Who else is in it? / Who cares? Mel Gibson!
jokes.setup: Family discovering movie tickets feature Mel Gibson
jokes.punchline: Marge's complete obsession overriding any other considerations
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's celebrity crush overriding rational thought.

jokes.text: Were you or Lenny ever named 'Sexiest Man Alive'? / I'm not certain about Lenny
jokes.setup: Marge defending Mel Gibson's specialness
jokes.punchline: Homer genuinely uncertain about Lenny's sex appeal credentials
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - completely sincere uncertainty about Lenny's potential sex appeal. Great character comedy.

jokes.text: Homer, let's make love. / Uh, uh, okay.
jokes.setup: Marge getting excited about Mel Gibson
jokes.punchline: Direct transition to bedroom activities, Homer's awkward acceptance
jokes.explanation: Adult humor handled with typical Simpsons restraint. Homer's hesitant agreement is funny.

jokes.text: You're thinking about me, right? / Of course, Homie. Aren't you thinking about me? / I will now!
jokes.setup: Intimate moment with Homer seeking reassurance
jokes.punchline: Homer admitting he wasn't thinking about Marge until she asked
jokes.explanation: Perfect relationship humor - Homer's honesty at the worst possible moment.

jokes.text: the dynamic duo, William Milo and Robin Hannah, who green-lighted all of Shaquille O'Neal's movies, including Kazaam
jokes.setup: Introducing successful Hollywood executives
jokes.punchline: Their claim to fame is greenlighting terrible Shaq movies
jokes.explanation: Good Hollywood satire - presenting obviously bad career moves as achievements.

jokes.text: How's the popcorn, guys? / Needs salt. / [Laughing]
jokes.setup: Executive trying to make small talk with Hollywood bigwigs
jokes.punchline: Mundane response gets disproportionate laughter
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Hollywood's fake enthusiasm over nothing.

jokes.text: Will there be any flubber in this movie? / Glavin. / No, I'm afraid not. / Ah, for crying out glavin.
jokes.setup: Q&A session about serious political drama
jokes.punchline: Frink asking about children's movie element, using his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink moment - completely inappropriate question delivered with his signature verbal tics.

jokes.text: John Travolta flew me in his jet. Now I have to help him move next weekend. He deliberately waited till we were in the air to ask me.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he got to Springfield so fast
jokes.punchline: Travolta's manipulative timing for asking favors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant celebrity humor - the relatable experience of being trapped into commitments, amplified by private jet setting.

jokes.text: Oh, Robin, you've got to see the director's cut of Booty Call. It's fabulous! / What, even better than the original? 'Cause that was pretty fabulous too.
jokes.setup: Hollywood executives discussing their expertise
jokes.punchline: Treating lowbrow comedy as high art with 'fabulous' repetition
jokes.explanation: Good Hollywood satire showing executives' pretentious treatment of commercial trash.

jokes.text: At least the Jimmy Stewart version had that giant rabbit who ran the savings and loan.
jokes.setup: Homer critiquing the boring political drama
jokes.punchline: Confusing Mr. Smith Goes to Washington with Harvey and It's a Wonderful Life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer cultural illiteracy - mashing together multiple Jimmy Stewart movies into nonsense.

jokes.text: It was nice to see a movie where people solve their problems with words, instead of bullets and chasing. / Oh, you're just saying that because your boyfriend was in it.
jokes.setup: Marge giving thoughtful film criticism
jokes.punchline: Homer reducing it to jealousy over her celebrity crush
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamic - Homer's insecurity undermining Marge's legitimate point.

jokes.text: Listen, I don't want to get spotted by the crowd, so I'm gonna take a leak behind the Dumpster.
jokes.setup: Movie star trying to avoid attention
jokes.punchline: Choosing the least dignified possible hiding spot
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity deflation humor - glamorous star in unglamorous situation.

jokes.text: [Alarm Ringing] Look! It's Mel Gibson! Hey, everybody, rush over there!
jokes.setup: Homer tries to avoid crowd by going behind dumpster
jokes.punchline: Immediately spotted and causes exactly what he wanted to avoid
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal. Homer's plan backfires immediately. Solid structure but predictable outcome.

jokes.text: Well, I'll be reading yours personally. [Growls]
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Mel will read opinion cards himself
jokes.punchline: Mel's intense, threatening response to Homer specifically
jokes.explanation: Good setup for what's coming - Mel's intimidating focus on Homer foreshadows their partnership.

jokes.text: Waste my time in front of this stupid- Appreciate it.
jokes.setup: Homer angrily writing his review
jokes.punchline: Immediately switching to polite when Mel approaches
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's two-faced nature when confronted.

jokes.text: Loved it despite absence of flubber. Glavin.
jokes.setup: Reading positive review cards
jokes.punchline: Even Frink's positive review mentions his flubber obsession
jokes.explanation: Good callback maintaining Frink's character consistency even in background gag.

jokes.text: Your movie was more boring than church. All you did was yak, yak, yak. You didn't even shoot anybody.
jokes.setup: Reading Homer's brutal honest review
jokes.punchline: Reducing sophisticated political drama to lack of violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer criticism - completely missing the point of prestige filmmaking.

jokes.text: Turn the plane around. I want to go to 742 Evergreen Terrace. / But you promised to help me move.
jokes.setup: Mel deciding to track down his critic
jokes.punchline: Travolta still focused on the moving favor
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Travolta's single-minded focus on his selfish needs.

jokes.text: Knock his teeth out, Homer. / Listen, Gibson, I'm tired of Hollywood pretty boys like you and Jack Valenti thinking you can have any woman you want.
jokes.setup: Homer confronting Mel Gibson at his door
jokes.punchline: Including MPAA head Jack Valenti as a Hollywood heartthrob
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - Jack Valenti as a sex symbol is wonderfully random and specific.

jokes.text: You see this? It symbolizes that she's my property, and I own her.
jokes.setup: Homer showing his wedding ring
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding marriage symbolism
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Homer's neanderthal attitude toward marriage.

jokes.text: Don't sell your husband short, Mrs. Simpson. / She's always doing that, Mel.
jokes.setup: Mel defending Homer's capabilities
jokes.punchline: Homer agreeing he's underappreciated despite being obviously incompetent
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's complete lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: I speed all the time, but cops never give me a ticket. If I don't pay my taxes, the IRS pays them for me.
jokes.setup: Mel explaining his problem with celebrity privilege
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd examples of special treatment
jokes.explanation: Good escalating examples of celebrity privilege taken to logical extreme.

jokes.text: You had me at 'hello.' / I didn't say 'hello.'
jokes.setup: Homer using Jerry Maguire reference inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Mel's literal correction
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference subversion. Gets the job done but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: A minivan! Oh, boy, you celebrities sure know how to live. / What is this, Toyota Previa? / Dodge Caravan. / Sweet.
jokes.setup: Homer expecting celebrity luxury
jokes.punchline: Enthusiasm for mundane family vehicle
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's easily impressed nature.

jokes.text: Robert Downey Jr. is shooting it out with the police. / I don't see any cameras.
jokes.setup: Tour guide pointing out movie filming
jokes.punchline: Tourist realizing it's not a movie but actual crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor playing on RDJ's real-life legal troubles. Perfect Hollywood satire.

jokes.text: [Whispering] Mel's movie really stinks.
jokes.setup: Homer is supposed to help improve the movie
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults it again behind Mel's back
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent duplicity from Homer. Can't help being honest even when he's trying to help.

jokes.text: Here you need a musical montage where you try on lots of funny hats. It'll let us see your playful side. / No. / Yeah, but- / Just no.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting terrible movie clichés
jokes.punchline: Mel's increasingly firm rejection
jokes.explanation: Good dynamic showing artistic integrity vs. commercial pandering.

jokes.text: That's where it used to be.
jokes.setup: Tour guide promising famous Brown Derby restaurant
jokes.punchline: It no longer exists at the location
jokes.explanation: Good economy of humor - perfect deflation of tourist expectations in minimal words.

jokes.text: And on your left, is the notorious spot where Hugh Grant- / Ew! / filmed the movie Nine Months. / Ew!
jokes.setup: Guide mentions Hugh Grant's 'notorious spot' (implying scandal)
jokes.punchline: It's just where he filmed a bad movie, tourists still disgusted
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up scandal reference but delivers movie reference. Nine Months was genuinely bad enough to warrant disgust.

jokes.text: You want me to replace the villain with a dog? / They will if you set up that the dog is evil. All you have to do is show him doing this- [Hums dramatic theme]
jokes.setup: Homer's ridiculous story suggestion
jokes.punchline: Explaining how dramatic music can make anything villainous
jokes.explanation: Good film industry satire showing how music manipulation works in movies.

jokes.text: The country was doing great back then. Everyone was into talking. But now, in whatever year this is, the audience wants action.
jokes.setup: Homer critiquing the filibuster scene
jokes.punchline: Not even knowing what year it is while making cultural commentary
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor and character comedy - Homer making sweeping cultural observations while being completely clueless.

jokes.text: It's the most boring piece of garbage I've ever seen. And it's not easy for me to say that.
jokes.setup: Homer delivering harsh criticism
jokes.punchline: Claiming it's difficult when he's obviously enjoying the brutality
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's false modesty while delivering insults.

jokes.text: According to the map, this house is owned by the dog from Frasier.
jokes.setup: Hollywood tour pointing out celebrity homes
jokes.punchline: Dog character owning real estate
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor playing with celebrity culture extending to animals.

jokes.text: According to the map, this house is owned by the dog from Frasier.
jokes.setup: Family is touring celebrity homes
jokes.punchline: TV show dog owns real estate
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about celebrity culture extending to animals. Economical but not particularly deep.

jokes.text: We're lesbians!
jokes.setup: Tour pointing out Ellen and Anne's house
jokes.punchline: Characters shouting their relationship status
jokes.explanation: Dated reference humor that relies on shock value rather than genuine wit.

jokes.text: Yield this, Senator Payne. [Grunts]
jokes.setup: Political scene about yielding the floor
jokes.punchline: Using 'yield' as action movie one-liner before violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay combining political terminology with action movie violence.

jokes.text: I move we impose some serious term limits. / I second that motion, with a vengeance!
jokes.setup: Political procedural language during action sequence
jokes.punchline: Parliamentary procedure meets Die Hard reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay combining constitutional procedure with action movie clichés. Very clever writing.

jokes.text: All in favor, say 'Die.'
jokes.setup: Parliamentary voting procedure
jokes.punchline: Replacing 'aye' with 'die' as violence escalates
jokes.explanation: Perfect wordplay with maximum economy. Simple substitution creates brilliant dark humor.

jokes.text: Happy birthday, Mr. President.
jokes.setup: After decapitating the President
jokes.punchline: Marilyn Monroe reference in completely inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor juxtaposing famous seductive moment with presidential murder.

jokes.text: You chopped off the president's head. / Bet you didn't see that coming.
jokes.setup: Executive's horrified reaction to presidential decapitation
jokes.punchline: Homer proud of the surprise twist
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's pride in shock value over storytelling.

jokes.text: It was symbolism. He was mad!
jokes.setup: Asked to explain the senseless violence
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-intellectual justification
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of pretentious film analysis. Homer's explanation is perfectly meaningless.

jokes.text: Ew! Those movies sucked. I only saw them to get Marge into the sack. P.S. Mission accomplished.
jokes.setup: Comparing to prestigious art films
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude motivation and unnecessary overshare
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overshare. In character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: This is worse than Godfather III. / Whoa, whoa. Hey, whoa. Let's not say things we can't take back.
jokes.setup: Executive comparing to notoriously bad sequel
jokes.punchline: Mel treating it as genuinely hurtful comparison
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing even Mel has limits to criticism he'll accept.

jokes.text: You put me down, you big lummox! / Jed! / Shut up, old lady. And stop kicking me there!
jokes.setup: Action star filming with elderly actress
jokes.punchline: Beverly Hillbillies reference with inappropriate action movie treatment
jokes.explanation: Good Hollywood satire combining nostalgic TV with modern action sensibilities.

jokes.text: I'm getting too old for this crap. / How old are you anyway? / Well, I've been told I can play anywhere from 28 to- / Sorry I asked.
jokes.setup: Action movie cliché followed by age question
jokes.punchline: Homer cutting off Mel's obviously vain response
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity vanity humor with Homer's practical response.

jokes.text: I'm getting too old for this crap. / How old are you anyway? / Well, I've been told I can play anywhere from 28 to- / Sorry I asked.
jokes.setup: Mel uses classic action movie line about being too old
jokes.punchline: Reveals vanity about age range instead of actual age, Homer cuts him off
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing celebrity vanity. Homer's interruption prevents the boasting from continuing.

jokes.text: Turn that Down Under frown upside down! We're not finished yet!
jokes.setup: Mel giving up on their mission
jokes.punchline: Homer's encouraging pun on Australian reference
jokes.explanation: Decent pun combining encouragement with Australian reference.

jokes.text: No time talk. Need steal car. Must save powerful but controversial movie.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining their desperate situation
jokes.punchline: Speaking like caveman while describing artsy film goals
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between primitive communication and sophisticated film concepts.

jokes.text: Movies aren't stupid! They fill us with romance and hatred and revenge fantasies. Lethal Weapon showed us that suicide is funny.
jokes.setup: Homer defending the importance of movies
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding Lethal Weapon's message about suicide
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor showing Homer's complete misinterpretation of serious themes. Perfectly in character.

jokes.text: That really wasn't my intention.
jokes.setup: Homer's interpretation of Lethal Weapon's suicide themes
jokes.punchline: Mel's horrified reaction to having his work misunderstood
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight man response to Homer's dark misinterpretation.

jokes.text: Before Lethal Weapon 2, I never thought there could be a bomb in my toilet. But now I check every time. / It's true. He does.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining movies' impact on his worldview
jokes.punchline: Lisa confirming this bizarre paranoid behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Lisa's confirmation making it even funnier.

jokes.text: They're my only escape from the drudgery of work and family. / No offense. / Ah, what the heck.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining movies' importance to him
jokes.punchline: Casually insulting his family life, weak apology, Marge's resignation
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor showing Homer's tactlessness and Marge's resigned acceptance.

jokes.text: Oh, sorry, honey. I used up the last roll on that man I thought was Judge Judy.
jokes.setup: Bart wants his picture taken in celebrity car
jokes.punchline: Marge wasted film photographing wrong celebrity
jokes.explanation: Mild celebrity confusion joke. Shows tourist desperation to photograph anyone who might be famous.

jokes.text: Oh, no! We killed Mel Gibson! / You all saw it. He came at me with a knife, right?
jokes.setup: Homer thinks they killed Mel Gibson in crash
jokes.punchline: Immediately constructs self-defense alibi involving imaginary knife
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's panic response. The instant fabrication of self-defense story is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Oh, no! We killed Mel Gibson! / You all saw it. He came at me with a knife, right?
jokes.setup: Apparent death of celebrity
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately trying to establish self-defense narrative
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's instant self-preservation instincts.

jokes.text: I know, but he sells tickets.
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out it's just a dummy
jokes.punchline: Homer's pragmatic assessment of Mel's box office value
jokes.explanation: Good Hollywood satire reducing actors to their commercial value.

jokes.text: Did Braveheart run away? Did Payback run away?
jokes.setup: Homer inspiring Mel for final confrontation
jokes.punchline: Confusing actor with his movie characters
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity confusion humor - treating roles as real identity.

jokes.text: Your army mooned the enemy until they could take no more and surrendered. / No, they didn't. They attacked us in a horribly bloody battle. Remember? / Actually, I didn't see it.
jokes.setup: Homer's battle strategy from Braveheart
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong about the movie he never actually watched
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation revealing Homer never saw the movie he's referencing. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: It's your plan- from Braveheart. Your army mooned the enemy until they could take no more and surrendered. / No, they didn't. They attacked us in a horribly bloody battle. Remember? / Actually, I didn't see it.
jokes.setup: Homer references Braveheart battle strategy
jokes.punchline: Completely misremembers the plot, admits he never actually watched it
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer moment - confidently wrong about movie plot, then admits he never saw it. Perfect escalation and character consistency.

jokes.text: There's no room in today's wacky, misty-eyed America for violent dinosaurs like us. / How did the country lose its way, Mel? When did we stop rooting for the man with the flamethrower, or an acid-spraying gun of some kind?
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting the loss of violent entertainment
jokes.punchline: Specific, absurd weapons preferences
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Homer's specific weapon preferences revealing his mindset.

jokes.text: How about a teen sex romp where you and your buddies are always trying to get some? / No, wait! A ghost who wins the lottery. You could be the ghost or the lottery commissioner.
jokes.setup: Homer pitching movie ideas
jokes.punchline: Increasingly random and terrible concepts
jokes.explanation: Good Hollywood satire showing terrible high-concept pitches.

jokes.text: Hey, Mel! I fell out!
jokes.setup: Action sequence ending
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual announcement of falling from vehicle
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with Homer's matter-of-fact delivery.

tmdb_overview: Homer befriends Mel Gibson just as Mel is completing a remake of Mr. Smith Goes To Washington. Homer convinces Mel to make the film more violent, with disastrous results.
